name: Awakening: Void
text:
They're looking for you.

You can hear their boots on the cobblestones. Three of them — heavy, confident, spreading out to cover the exits. Torchlight scrapes the walls of the alley, orange and hungry. You're pressed into the corner behind the rain barrel, making yourself as small as you can.

You don't know what you did to make them angry. That's not true — you know exactly what you did. You just didn't think they'd find out.

The torchlight sweeps toward your corner. You can see the shadow of the man holding it, stretched long and wrong on the opposite wall. Three more steps and he'll see you.

You wish — desperately, completely, with every part of yourself — to not be here.

And then you're not.

You're still standing in the corner. Your feet are on the same cobblestones. Your hand is on the same rain barrel. But the torchlight passes over you like water over a stone. The man walks within arm's reach and his eyes slide past without catching. You're not invisible — you're irrelevant. The world has forgotten you're here, and the forgetting is so complete that even the light doesn't bother.

They search for twenty minutes. You stand in the open for most of it, three feet from a man who is actively looking for you, and he never once sees you.

When they leave, you're shaking. Not from fear — from the absence of it. You should be terrified. Instead you feel hollow and calm and absolutely safe, and you don't understand how something that feels like disappearing can feel so much like coming home.
